input.headline: The Invisible Toll: How Warehouse Injuries Go Unreported
input.summary: An investigation revealing that major warehouse operators systematically underreport workplace injuries by classifying them as pre-existing conditions. Based on OSHA records, internal documents, and interviews with 40 workers.
input.arresting_line: For every injury that makes it into the official count, workers say three more are quietly reclassified or ignored.

input.headline: City Council Approves New Bike Lane Network
input.summary: The council voted 7-2 to approve a $28 million bike lane expansion covering 45 miles of city roads over three years.

input.headline: The Brussels Effect: How EU Tech Rules Are Reshaping Silicon Valley
input.summary: Feature on how the Digital Services Act and AI Act are forcing US tech companies to redesign products globally rather than maintain separate EU versions. Includes interviews with compliance officers at three major firms and analysis of the regulatory divergence between EU and US approaches.
input.arresting_line: The cheapest way to comply with EU law, it turns out, is to comply everywhere.

description: Adversarial: inherently dry topic that needs a compelling teaser

input.headline: Municipal Bond Refinancing Rates Hit a 12-Year Inflection Point
input.summary: A detailed analysis of how shifting municipal bond refinancing rates are affecting local government infrastructure spending. Explains the mechanics of advance refunding, current refunding windows, and how a 40-basis-point shift has caused 23 mid-size cities to delay planned water and sewer upgrades. Heavy on bond market data.

description: South Asian: investigative piece on media independence

input.headline: The Editors Who Said No: Press Freedom Under Pressure in South Asia
input.summary: Investigation into how media owners in a South Asian democracy are pressuring editors to suppress stories critical of the ruling party. Based on interviews with 15 former editors and internal communications from three major media groups. Documents a pattern of advertiser boycotts coordinated through government intermediaries.
input.arresting_line: The phone call always comes at night, always from a number the editor does not recognize, and the message is always the same: kill the story.

description: Latin American: feature on environmental defenders

input.headline: Guardians Under Fire: The Deadly Cost of Protecting Latin America's Forests
input.summary: Feature on environmental defenders in Central and South America who face death threats and violence for opposing illegal logging and mining operations. Profiles three activists from different countries, examines the failure of state protection programs, and analyzes data showing 180 environmental defenders killed in the region over the past five years.
input.arresting_line: She carries a notebook with the names of 14 colleagues who were killed. She keeps a blank page at the end.

description: A/B testing: same story needing two distinct teaser variants

input.headline: Why Your City's Tap Water Might Not Be as Safe as You Think
input.summary: Investigation revealing that 1 in 5 municipal water systems in the country have not updated their lead pipe inventories as required by new federal regulations. Includes interactive database where readers can check their own zip code. Based on EPA compliance data and interviews with water utility directors.
input.arresting_line: The pipes under your street were installed before your grandparents were born. Nobody has checked them since.
